name: Community Trust
description_short: Self-Help Federal Credit Union is a mission-driven credit union with branches in CA, CT, IL, SC, WA, and WI focused on economic opportunity for underserved communities.
description_long:
Self-Help Federal Credit Union is a community development financial institution (CDFI) credit union operating under routing number 322273696. It serves as the federal charter arm of the broader Self-Help family, which also includes a separate credit union for members in North Carolina, Florida, Virginia, Georgia, and South Carolina. Its stated mission is 'creating and protecting ownership and economic opportunity for all, especially people of color, women, rural residents and low-wealth families and communities.'

Self-Help Federal offers a full suite of personal banking products including savings, checking, money market accounts (1.64% APY as of March 2026), and certificates of deposit — a 1-year certificate at 3.82% APY and a 3-year certificate at 3.35% APY. On the lending side, members can access home loans, auto loans (with rate discounts for electric and plug-in hybrid vehicles), small business loans, and commercial loans. COVID business resources remain available. All members get online and mobile banking access, and free one-on-one financial coaching is offered as a standard benefit.

What distinguishes Self-Help Federal from conventional banks and credit unions is its explicit focus on borrowers that mainstream institutions routinely decline. The credit union uses personalized loan underwriting — evaluating individual circumstances rather than relying solely on credit scores — to broaden access to homeownership and small business financing for low-income and minority borrowers. Free financial coaching, scholarship programs for community-active students, and a remote-services infrastructure that lets members join regardless of geography further reinforce its community development mandate.

Self-Help Federal is a strong option for mission-aligned banking and for borrowers who want deposits channeled into community development lending rather than shareholder returns. CD rates are competitive and financial coaching is a genuine differentiator. That said, branch access is limited to six states, and the money market rate of 1.64% APY trails high-yield online banks by a wide margin. During research the FAQ page returned a 404 error and the homepage displayed a browser security warning, minor signals of an aging digital infrastructure. Like all credit unions, membership eligibility requirements apply before accounts can be opened. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Free one-on-one financial coaching available to all members at no charge
1-year certificate at 3.82% APY is competitive against national credit union averages (as of March 2026)
Personalized loan underwriting that looks beyond credit scores for homebuyers and small business borrowers
Discounted auto loan rates for electric and plug-in hybrid vehicle purchases
Remote/online membership available nationwide, not limited to branch states
Annual scholarship program open to community-active students
Explicit CDFI mission — deposits fund loans to underserved borrowers, not investor dividends
cons: Physical branches only in six states (CA, CT, IL, SC, WA, WI) — limited in-person access
Money market rate of 1.64% APY significantly trails high-yield online bank competitors
FAQ page returns a 404 error, indicating gaps in digital support infrastructure
Website displayed a browser compatibility warning, suggesting slower front-end maintenance cycles
Membership eligibility requirements apply — not open-access like a bank
best_for: Borrowers of color, women, or low-income applicants who face rejection at conventional banks
Community-minded savers who want deposits used for local economic development
Small business owners in underserved markets seeking flexible underwriting
Residents of CA, CT, IL, SC, WA, or WI wanting a full-service credit union with a social mission
diagnosis: Self-Help Federal Credit Union is best for consumers and small business owners who have been underserved by conventional lenders — particularly people of color, women, rural residents, and low-wealth households — who want a full-service credit union that prioritizes their community. The main caveat is limited branch coverage (six states) and a money market rate that lags competitive online alternatives, so members purely optimizing for yield should compare elsewhere.
services: Certificates of deposit — 1-year (3.82% APY) and 3-year (3.35% APY)
Auto loans with EV rate discounts
